mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, equity valuations face downward pressure from deteriorating consumer income expectations and elevated recession risk, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series expand, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a liquidity-driven recovery phase indicates improving capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during liquidity-driven recovery phases, or the mechanism's predictive power vanishes outside this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, extreme monetary accommodation eliminates downside risk from financing costs and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain negative or flat when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total employment rises while the unemployment rate falls confirms genuine labor market tightening, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP during an early recovery phase signal rapid demand restoration, prompting algorithmic and momentum traders to bid up equities, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth surprises fail to generate positive 5-day returns during early recovery phases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rebounds above 95.0 percent of its pre-recession baseline, it confirms supply chain stabilization and removes recession fears, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 95.0, or the threshold crossing loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample validation.
